docs.title: Who Am I?
docs.text: Software Engineer based in the Pacific Northwest, with a special interest in User Interface Design and Database Internals.

docs.title: What am I currently obsessed with?
docs.text:
• Astral — They've got the Midas touch right now, their entire suite of Rust-based tools is pure gold. If you haven't switched to uv yet, you're sleeping. Let uv manage dependencies & virtual environments, ruff for linting, and ty handle your type checking at absurd speeds. Genuinely can't remember what life was like before this, productivity and code quality have increased tenfold.

  • Apache Arrow's PyArrow — I'm usually a heavy Pandas user but PyArrow has kind of stolen the show. The type system is way more rigorous, the in-memory columnar storage is incredibly efficient. It seems Pandas is leaning towards fully adopting PyArrow very soon, but either way it's been a game changer for all tabular work.

  • Apache DataFusion — a query engine written in Rust that I'm definitely obsessed with. When you dig into how it works, you start to appreciate its technical excellence. The gap between "here's what you asked for" and "here's the most efficient way to do it" is where a huge amount of engineering lives — predicate pushdown, projection pruning, join reordering — and DataFusion does all this with a clarity and performance profile that feels like a glimpse at where the next generation of data systems is heading.

  • SQLModel — This one's become my go-to for anything database related. It sits on top of SQLAlchemy and Pydantic which means you get proper data validation basically for free, and writing async APIs that handle database transactions safely feels almost too easy. Less boilerplate, fewer bugs, happier me.

Just a glimpse of some of my favorite tools right now.

docs.title: Personal
docs.text:
You’re most likely here with an interest in my technical background, but I’ll gladly share a few personal tidbits too.

  • I love playing the Piano and studying music theory, it's something I picked up a few years ago and helps me get away from the screens.
  • I'm a competitive Olympic Weightlifter! I competed for four years throughout University, and even made two National Championships! Though I do still enjoy slinging the barbell, I am actually currently training for a half-marathon.
  • Since I am located in the Pacific Northwest, of course I enjoy connecting with nature through various outdoor activities, a way to "tough grass" if you will.

docs.title: LandscAIpeTech Stack
docs.text:
LandscAIpe is an Expo/React Native mobile application for AI-powered landscaping design and contractor matching. Live Now on the app store! It targets iOS and Android, backed by Supabase (PostgreSQL + Edge Functions + Storage) and Google Gemini for image generation.|

MobileExpo 54, React Native 0.81, React 19 LanguageTypeScript (src/) + JavaScript (legacy screens/) BackendSupabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, Storage, Edge Functions) AI / Image GenGoogle Gemini AnalyticsPostHog (events + session tracking) Error TrackingSentry (with session replay) PaymentsStripe (contractor billing), react-native-iap (DIY Pro) PushExpo Notifications AuthEmail/Password, Google Sign-In, Apple Sign-In

docs.title: DataFusion Plan Visualizer
docs.text:
• An interactive web application for visualizing query plans produced by Apache DataFusion. Write SQL against uploaded tables and inspect how DataFusion's query planner decomposes it into a tree of relational operators — across all four plan representations.
  • Live Demo
  • Github Repository
Query EngineApache DataFusion Columnar FormatApache Arrow via PyArrow Data Wranglingpandas 3.0 Async API LayerFastAPI + Uvicorn Graph Renderingcytoscape.js DeploymentVercel

docs.title: WIP: JrLytics
docs.text:
• A mini OLAP database engine built in Python. JrLytics implements segment-based columnar storage with metadata-driven query optimization on top of Apache DataFusion.
  • Github Repository
